title: ozoroa cinerea
intro: Ozoroa cinerea, also known as the White Milkwood, is a species of plant in the family Anacardiaceae, native to southern Africa. The plant's bark and leaves have been traditionally used in herbal medicine for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Ozoroa cinerea include its ability to treat fever, cough, and respiratory infections, as well as its use as a treatment for skin conditions such as eczema and acne. Ozoroa cinerea is typically prepared in herbal remedies such as infusions, decoctions, and ointments, which are used to extract its medicinal properties and provide relief from various health issues.
